title: Methi Malai Matar

ingredients.name: fresh methi (fenugreek) leaves, washed and roughly chopped
visual_checks: about 150 g leaves stripped from stems
ingredients.notes: about 150 g leaves stripped from stems

subs.name: baby spinach if methi is unavailable; the dish loses its characteristic slight bitterness but remains delicious
diced paneer or corn for a different but complementary texture

ingredients.name: cashew nuts
visual_checks: soaked 20 minutes in warm water, then blended smooth with 4 tbsp water
ingredients.notes: soaked 20 minutes in warm water, then blended smooth with 4 tbsp water

subs.name: 2 tbsp thick coconut milk instead of cashew paste; add with the cream
full-fat evaporated milk or thick coconut cream for a lighter result

steps.text: To reduce the bitterness of the methi, sprinkle the chopped leaves with a little salt and leave for 10 minutes, then squeeze out any released water. This step is important — it makes the curry pleasant rather than sharp-bitter.
Soak the cashews in hot water for 20 minutes, then drain and blend with 4 tbsp water into a completely smooth paste. Set aside.

Heat oil in a kadai over medium flame. Add cardamom, cloves and bay leaf; let them sizzle for 30 seconds. Add the onion and cook until soft and just beginning to turn golden, about 5 minutes — you do not want a dark brown base here since the gravy should be pale.
Add the ginger-garlic paste and green chilli and cook 1 minute. Add the methi leaves and toss over medium-high heat for 3–4 minutes until they wilt and cook down significantly.
Add the green peas and cook 3 minutes. Stir in the cashew paste and cook on medium-low for 4 minutes until the raw cashew smell disappears and the sauce is fragrant.

Pour in the cream, milk and 100 ml hot water. Add sugar, garam masala and salt; stir and simmer gently for 5 minutes until everything is combined and the sauce is creamy. Taste and adjust salt and sugar. Serve with naan or jeera rice.

cross_contact_notes: Ingredient metadata cannot prove an allergen-free kitchen. Check packaged labels and cross-contact statements for your products.
trust.provenance_summary: AI-assisted original draft; declared licence original.
trust.substitution_warning: A substitution can introduce allergens or change dietary suitability. Never claim a swap is safe without checking the replacement's label and the user's allergy context.

substitution_notes: fenugreek-leaves → baby spinach if methi is unavailable; the dish loses its characteristic slight bitterness but remains delicious; cashew → 2 tbsp thick coconut milk instead of cashew paste; add with the cream; cream → full-fat evaporated milk or thick coconut cream for a lighter result; peas → diced paneer or corn for a different but complementary texture
